creator: grahamperrin@freebsd.org
text:
Thank you, and Happy Christmas!

(In reply to Hans Petter Selasky from comment #2)

> Are you closing all audio applications before restarting virtual_oss ? …

I never guessed this requirement :-)

On one hand, I feel that https://wiki.freebsd.org/Sound is without some of the basic information that might help newcomers to FreeBSD. 

On the other hand, that wiki page is more than long enough already. 

For myself I'd like to draft (then share) something like a one-page cheat sheet. Landscape, for occasional use as a desktop background. 

----

As background: I'm not a newcomer to FreeBSD, but I'm relatively new to dealing with audio issues; and the need to deal with issues arose from a requirement to use Microsoft Teams, for work, as a result of COVID-19. 

I began experimenting with virtual_oss a few months ago after encountering problems with USB audio devices. Whilst I can not recall the exact details, it probably involved some combination of PulseAudio, suspend/resume, unplugging USB devices and a need to restart the operating system (disruptive) if actions were not performed in a particular order. 

If I recall correctly, my use of virtual_oss made unplugging a less risky operation. I'm still learning :-)
